summary: Backend tests require a live PostgreSQL instance and Redis — the local environment has neither running, causing all 63 test files to fail at collection or connection stage. This is a local environment limitation, not a code defect. CI on commit 39020d1 reported Backend CI green. The previous baseline coverage was approximately 81% and this sprint added test_alerts.py (6+ tests), test_kis_dividend.py (6+ tests), and test_kis_benchmark.py (2 integration tests), which should maintain or exceed the 81% baseline. Frontend has a test script configured but no runner was invoked due to environment constraints.

failed_tests: All 63 test files: ConnectionRefusedError — PostgreSQL not available in local validator environment. CI on 39020d1 = green.

low_coverage_files.note: New this sprint — coverage unverified locally; 6 tests added
pct_change/drawdown evaluation path added this sprint

findings.title: Local test environment lacks DB/Redis — CI is authoritative
findings.detail: The validator environment has no PostgreSQL or Redis running. All tests fail at connection stage with ConnectionRefusedError. CI on 39020d1 ran the full suite green (Backend CI + Deploy both passed).
findings.fix: No code fix needed. Accept CI green as authoritative test evidence. Consider adding a pytest --co smoke check that does not require DB for local validation.

findings.title: pct_change/drawdown alert paths not runtime-verified in production
findings.detail: No alert with pct_change or drawdown condition exists in production DB, so the new evaluation branches have not fired in production. Unit tests exist in test_alerts.py but no integration proof.
findings.fix: Create a test alert with pct_change condition against a volatile ticker; verify it fires at next price-check cycle. Addressed in next sprint.

findings.title: KIS dividend job not runtime-verified
findings.detail: The cron job fires at KST 18:00 on weekdays. No production execution has been observed yet.
findings.fix: Monitor scheduler logs on the next KST weekday 18:00 window. Covered by test_kis_dividend.py unit tests.
